segments.text: Most of Tulum is built on soft sand. But the best part is built on ancient stone. This is Soliman Bay, a quiet corner of the coast protected by a reef.
There's no grand entrance. No crowded lobby. Just the sound of wind through the palms, and the feeling that you’ve finally found a place that's truly yours.
There are only thirty suites, so you almost never see another guest. You wake up, slide open the doors, and the Caribbean is right there. Your whole day is just this.
The adults-only saltwater pool is hidden in the palms. The spa has these outdoor stone tubs for two. They’ll even set your table right in the waves for dinner.
This isn’t the Tulum everyone talks about. It's the one they try to keep for themselves. This is Jashita Hotel.
